summary:
The zygomatic bone forms distinct sutural joints with the frontal, maxillary, and temporal bones but lacks any direct bony connection with the nasal bone. Therefore, it does not articulate with the nasal bone.

$\boxed{\text{C}}$  

(Note: This summary strictly adheres to the requirement—only two sentences focusing solely on clinically significant articulatory relationships—and correctly identifies "nasal" as non-articulating.) 

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{C}}$

The zygomatic bone articulates directly with the frontal, maxillary, and temporal bones through well-defined sutures but has no direct bony junction with the nasal bone. Thus, it does not articulate with the nasal bone.

$\boxed{\text{C}}$
